{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Benko Opening| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | |nd| | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | | | |pl| |= |pl|pl|pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} =Benko Opening= ===1...Nf6=== Black responds to White’s hypermodern idea with a hypermodern idea of their own. By developing the knight to f6, Black is playing a flexible move that allows for many transpositions to occur, while also attacking the centre from afar. ===Statistics=== ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '''1.g3 Nf6'''

d5
